course_title: Physiology of Systems & Clinical Application to Speech Pathology Practice

learning_outcomes.description: Describe general and specialised physiology of the cardiovascular, respiratory, gastrointestinal, sensory and motor nervous systems in healthy and aging populations and discuss how they relate to communication and swallowing.
Analyse physiological basis of diseases in individuals with communication and swallowing disorders.
Apply knowledge of pathophysiology to identify the clinical presentation of individuals with communication and swallowing disorders and evaluate pharmacological treatment options.
Apply techniques used in physiological experimentation, including hypothesis testing, methods of data collection and analysis, to evaluate experimental results.
Demonstrate collaborative learning skills and effective communication in a group setting.
Reflect and synthesise effectively on your learning in physiology.

assessments.due_date:
23/03/2026 - 27/03/2026

The quiz will open at 10:00 am on the Monday of Week 5 and close at 1:00 pm on the Friday of Week 5.
assessments.task_description:
For this assessment, the process will begin at the start of Week 2, when you will receive a detailed case study, along with a set of questions designed to deepen your understanding of biochemical processes (covered in Module 1) and the nervous system (covered in Module 2). These materials are intended to help you explore how these topics connect to specific health and disease scenarios. You will have the duration of these modules to formulate your responses.


The assessment itself will take place online via a 30-minute quiz on Blackboard, available in week 5. During the quiz, you will be presented with 3 to 4 of the previously provided questions to answer and submit. Please note that you will only have one opportunity to access and complete this quiz.


More details are provided in the 'Assessment' folder on Blackboard.


This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will complete this assessment through Blackboard within the date range.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
For this assessment, you will receive a case study that outlines a client's health situation. Your main task is to analyse the details of the case and synthesise this information into a detailed case report, which should

  • Clearly depict the client’s health conditions.
  • Demonstrate how different biological systems are interconnected in the client’s case.

Objectives:

  • This assessment is designed to enhance your understanding of the interactions between biological systems in health and disease contexts, helping you retain complex details, foster critical thinking, and promote collaborative learning.


Group Activity:

  • You will complete this task within a group setting.


Additional Support and Information:

  • Detailed information about this assessment will be provided during your Masterclasses and on Blackboard.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, with the electronic coversheet to Turnitin by the submission deadline.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
For Part C of your assessment, you'll explore the concluding chapter of the case. You will be required to integrate the knowledge acquired from Assessment Parts A and B with newly introduced biological systems. Collaborating within your group, you'll create and deliver a presentation responding to a specific prompt about the case study. This presentation should be recorded and submitted for marking. This assessment encourages the synthesis of complex information across different stages of learning and the development of communication skills and teamwork as you articulate and share your insights on the case study.


More details will be available on Blackboard.

assessments.task_description:
The end-of-semester exam in this course is designed to assess your comprehensive understanding of the covered material.

Exam Format:

  • Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The Library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard.
  • Question Types: Multiple-choice, short-answer, label the images, fill in the blanks, and other assorted questions
  • Content Coverage: The exam will cover all lectures, practical material, and modules, with the focus proportional to the number of lectures in each module.


Attendance at modules, workshops, and practical classes is highly recommended, as there will be explicit questions on these sections in the exam.

This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.


More details of the examination format will be provided on Blackboard during the semester.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The exam will be a closed book invigilated exam held on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The Library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera. Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Weekly Lectures

Each module is supported by weekly lectures followed by interactive activity sessions. Within these sessions, you will have activities to work through with your classmates to help you learn and/or consolidate your knowledge. You will also have an assessment in each (please see the assessment section). Your lecturers will facilitate these classes, allowing you some 1:1 time to discuss anything you are confused about.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 2(02 Mar - 08 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Biological foundations & homeostasis

Module 1 aims to establish a strong foundation for understanding key concepts in speech pathology. It starts with defining physiology and its importance in this field, then explores the chemical basics essential for biological functions. The focus then shifts to homeostasis, vital for bodily balance, encompassing negative and positive feedback mechanisms. We'll also examine cellular components, appreciating the complexity of organelles and how cells form tissues and organs. Concluding with an overview of major body systems, this module sets the stage for deeper, specialised discussions in subsequent modules, ensuring a comprehensive and integrated understanding for all students.

Associated lecture

  • Week 2: Introduction to the course
  • Week 3: Module 1 Content
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1: Biological foundations & homeostasis Module 1 aims to establish a strong foundation for understanding key concepts in speech pathology. The focus then shifts to homeostasis, vital for bodily balance, encompassing negative and positive feedback mechanisms. We'll also examine cellular components, appreciating the complexity of organelles and how cells form tissues and organs. Concluding with an overview of major body systems, this module sets the stage for deeper, specialised discussions in subsequent modules, ensuring a comprehensive and integrated understanding for all students. Associated lecture Week 2: Introduction to the course Week 3: Module 1 Content

learning_activities.topic:
Physiology Practical - Osmosis

The "Osmosis" practical demonstrates the mechanisms by which the fluids move within the body's compartments. This has important implications for how the body maintains fluid balance and the effect of introducing solutions (such as by intravenous injection) to the body.

This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Physiology Practical - Osmosis The "Osmosis" practical demonstrates the mechanisms by which the fluids move within the body's compartments. This has important implications for how the body maintains fluid balance and the effect of introducing solutions (such as by intravenous injection) to the body. This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 5(16 Mar - 29 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 2: The Nervous and Muscular System

Module 2 focuses on the brain, spinal cord, and muscle physiology. Understanding these areas is crucial for treating conditions like stuttering or post-stroke communication issues stemming from nervous system dysfunctions. The module covers basics to system-level understanding, including control of muscles and sensory feedback, and highlights neuro- and muscle physiology important for speech pathology. It delves into motor and sensory systems and movement disorders.

Associated Workshops:

  • Week 5: Activities will cover Module 2
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 2: The Nervous and Muscular System Module 2 focuses on the brain, spinal cord, and muscle physiology. Understanding these areas is crucial for treating conditions like stuttering or post-stroke communication issues stemming from nervous system dysfunctions. The module covers basics to system-level understanding, including control of muscles and sensory feedback, and highlights neuro- and muscle physiology important for speech pathology. It delves into motor and sensory systems and movement disorders. Associated Workshops: Week 5: Activities will cover Module 2

learning_activities.topic:
Physiology Practical - Skeletal Muscle

The "Skeletal Muscle" practical further examines muscle contraction, investigating how the strength of muscle contractions is controlled.
This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Physiology Practical  - Skeletal Muscle The "Skeletal Muscle" practical further examines muscle contraction, investigating how the strength of muscle contractions is controlled. This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.

learning_activities.topic:
Physiology Practical Action Potential [Online]

The "Action Potential" practical features a computer-based module designed to showcase the electrical properties of nerves. This activity can be completed at the student's own pace
learning_activities.topic_description: Physiology Practical  Action Potential [Online] The "Action Potential" practical features a computer-based module designed to showcase the electrical properties of nerves. This activity can be completed at the student's own pace

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 6 To Week 7(30 Mar - 19 Apr)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 3: The Cardiovascular system

The module begins by examining how oxygenated blood is pumped by the heart throughout the body and how deoxygenated blood carrying carbon dioxide returns to the lungs for gas exchange. Emphasizing their interdependent relationship, the module covers the mechanics of cardiac conduction and contraction and the process of air intake, oxygen transport, and carbon dioxide expulsion. While these systems are initially taught separately, their overlapping functions are highlighted.

Associated Workshops:

  • Week 7: Activities will cover Module 3
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 3: The Cardiovascular system The module begins by examining how oxygenated blood is pumped by the heart throughout the body and how deoxygenated blood carrying carbon dioxide returns to the lungs for gas exchange. Emphasizing their interdependent relationship, the module covers the mechanics of cardiac conduction and contraction and the process of air intake, oxygen transport, and carbon dioxide expulsion. While these systems are initially taught separately, their overlapping functions are highlighted. Associated Workshops: Week 7: Activities will cover Module 3

learning_activities.topic:
Physiology Practical - Spirometry

"Spirometry" is a practical session focused on measuring lung volumes and airflow, along with exploring the factors affecting these measurements.
This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Physiology Practical - Spirometry "Spirometry" is a practical session focused on measuring lung volumes and airflow, along with exploring the factors affecting these measurements. This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 8 To Week 9(20 Apr - 03 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 4: Respiration and the process of speech

This module focuses on the physiology of how we breathe, which is respiration. We will then relate this to articulation, resonation, and phonation in speech production.

Associated Workshops:

  • Week 11: Activities will cover Module 5
learning_activities.topic_title: Module 4: Respiration and the process of speech

We will then relate this to articulation, resonation, and phonation in speech production. Associated Workshops: Week 11: Activities will cover Module 5

learning_activities.topic:
Physiology Practical - Blood pressure

The "Blood Pressure" practical examines the measurement of arterial blood pressure and the effect of posture and stress on Blood Pressure. You will also investigate the venous circulation and listen to the heart sounds.
This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.

You will also investigate the venous circulation and listen to the heart sounds. This is completed on campus in the laboratory. Students are to remember to bring their PPE, lab coat, and closed-in shoes.

learning_activities.topic:
Physiology Practical : Respiration [Online]

"Respiration" is an online clinical scenario based on a respiratory case.
This activity can be completed at the student's own pace but should be finished by the end of this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Physiology Practical : Respiration [Online] "Respiration" is an online clinical scenario based on a respiratory case. This activity can be completed at the student's own pace but should be finished by the end of this week.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 10 To Week 11(04 May - 17 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 5: The Gastrointestinal system

In this module, you will learn about the gastrointestinal system, including the essential processes of ingestion, swallowing, motility, and mechanical and chemical digestion.

Associated Workshops:

  • Week 13: Activities will cover Module 6
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 5: The Gastrointestinal system In this module, you will learn about the gastrointestinal system, including the essential processes of ingestion, swallowing, motility, and mechanical and chemical digestion. Associated Workshops: Week 13: Activities will cover Module 6

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 12 To Week 13(18 May - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 6: Sensory Neurobiology, Mastication and Deglutition

In sensory neurobiology students will explore sensory reception, with a focus on sensory systems that are highly relevant to speech pathology – the auditory, gustatory, and olfactory systems. Whilst in the second part of this module students will focus on mastication and swallowing in depth. The goal of this final module is for students to integrate previous knowledge in neuroanatomy and physiology to obtain an advanced understanding of body processes.

Associated Workshops:

  • Week 12: Activities will cover Module 6.1
  • Week 13: Quiz or Module 5 & 6.1. Activities will cover Module 6.2

The goal of this final module is for students to integrate previous knowledge in neuroanatomy and physiology to obtain an advanced understanding of body processes.
